title: An Industrial Application of a Large Language Model to Enhancing Asset Integrity and Process Safety Management
journal: Journal of Artificial Intelligence, Virtual Reality, and Human-Centered Computing
abstract: A comparative study between two onshore liquefied natural gas plants; one utilizing AI-driven AIPS management and the other relying on manual data analysis indicates that AI-driven approaches significantly enhance the accuracy and speed of incident classifications, reducing data processing times.

title: The Influence of Investment Knowledge, Investment Motivation, Minimum Capital, Financial Literacy, Investment Risk, and Investment Return on Stock Investment Interest among University Students in Purwokerto
abstract: University students’ interest in stock investment remains varied despite the increasing accessibility of capital market participation through technological advancement and investment facilities. This condition indicates a gap between expanding investment opportunities and students’ readiness to engage in rational investment behaviour. This study aims to analyze the influence of investment knowledge, investment motivation, minimum capital, financial literacy, investment risk, and investment return on stock investment interest among university students in Purwokerto. A quantitative approach with a descriptive-explanatory design was employed, involving students from the Faculty of Economics and Business at Wijayakusuma University, Purwokerto, from the 2022–2024 cohorts. A total of 93 respondents were selected using proportionate stratified random sampling. Data were collected through Likert-scale questionnaires and analyzed using multiple linear regression, supported by classical assumption tests, the F-test, t-test, and coefficient of determination analysis. The findings reveal that investment knowledge, investment motivation, minimum capital, financial literacy, investment risk, and investment return positively and significantly influence students’ stock investment interest. Investment knowledge emerged as the most dominant variable, while the model explained 62.20% of the variation in investment interest. The study concludes that students’ stock investment interest is shaped by cognitive, psychological, financial, and evaluative factors. This study contributes to financial behaviour and capital market education literature by demonstrating the multidimensional determinants of student investment interest. The practical implications emphasize the need to strengthen investment education, financial literacy, and affordable investment access to encourage informed and sustainable student participation in stock investment activities.

title: T-tube duodenostomy with jejunal feeding tube placement for duodenal stump fistula: A case report and review of literature
abstract: BACKGROUND Duodenal stump fistula (DSF) is a rare but serious complication following gastrectomy, and its optimal treatment remains controversial. T-tube duodenostomy combined with jejunal feeding tube placement for the management of DSF has been rarely reported. CASE SUMMARY We report the case of a patient who underwent subtotal gastrectomy for pyloric stenosis. The duodenal stump drainage tube produced bile-like fluid 3 days after the operation, accompanied by sepsis. Emergency surgery confirmed the presence of a DSF. During the operation, he underwent T-tube duodenostomy combined with placement of a jejunal nutrition tube. He recovered and was discharged after 17 days of postoperative treatment. CONCLUSION This report provides clinicians with a surgical treatment option and ideas for prevention.

title: Reinterpreting identity in the context of space and reconciliation: A qualitative study on Arabized Kurds in the city center of Mardin
abstract: This study aims to understand, reveal and explore the hyphenated identities of the 'Arabized Kurds' living in Mardin city centre, considering the themes of space and reconciliation. The focus of this study is the question of how an identity, which is repositioned through the interrelation and actual transformation of two different ethnic and cultural identities, is formed through which encounters, interactions, and negotiation processes. This article presents data collected through qualitative research conducted using a case study design. Once again, it reveals that identity is not fixed or singular, but fluid and constantly reconstructed through space, language, and cultural interactions. The findings were interpreted through thematic and discourse analysis, using in-depth interviews and observation as the data collection tools. First, the Arabized Kurdish identity in Mardin demonstrates that ethnic boundaries are not rigid or insurmountable and can transform through historical processes and social contact. Secondly, this flexible, interconnected, hyphenated identity is much more effective than tolerance in establishing social reconciliation in Mardin because it manages social tensions by facilitating negotiation between ethnic groups.

title: Can audio production workshops nurture diversity in an increasingly uniform audio culture? The Flux Audio workshop philosophy
abstract: In this interview Abigail Wincott asks Julie Shapiro to reflect on the Audio Flux workshop she led at the Salt Institute for Documentary Studies in March 2026 and on her approach to audio production pedagogy. Julie expresses the teaching and learning philosophy of Audio Flux and the ways she attempts to 'ride the line' between clear instruction that saves time and responds to learners' wish to learn from experts, and 'cheerleading', giving permission to experiment freely and refraining from saying how things should be done. Teaching philosophies in audio production draw on and feed into cultures of experimentation, diversity and normativity in the creative industries. Though the workshop is based in the US, Julie seeks to deliberately distance her workshops from the dominant US storytelling style and argues that the three-minute format of Audio Flux opens up possibilities of adventurous listening as well as experimental making. The audio of the conversation is also available.

title: Urban forest remnants: key refuges for reptile conservation in the highlands of southern Mexico, local and landscape environmental factors effects
The Politics of the Home: How Family Labour Division, Patriarchy, and Kinship Reproduce Gender Inequality
abstract: This paper examines the family as a pivotal site in the production and reproduction of gender inequality. Tracing origins, continuation, and intergenerational transmission, it argues that household labour division and entrenched gender norms, which are rooted in biological determinist narratives, establish the initial asymmetry of roles. Contemporary dual-earner arrangements, while more common, often produce only superficial change: women continue to shoulder unpaid domestic and care work, generating a double burden. The essay locates the persistence of inequality in the dialectical relationship between patriarchy and economic dependence, drawing on Marxist and feminist frameworks (notably Mies) to show how unpaid domestic labour underpins capitalist reproduction and reinforces male dominance. Finally, it explores kinship structures and intersectional dimensions (class, race, and coloniality) and proposes queer kinship as a disruptive modality that can contest heteronormative inheritance of gendered labour. The study synthesises theory and empirical findings to offer conceptual and practical pathways towards substantive equality.

title: The Influence of Social Media Marketing on Purchase Decisions Moderated by Electronic Word of Mouth at Cititex Kupang
journal: Journal of Management and Business Innovation
abstract: This study aims to analyze the influence of social media marketing on purchasing decisions with electronic word of mouth as a moderating variable at Cititex Kupang, a fashion retailer operating in Kupang City, Indonesia. The research employed a quantitative method with an associative approach. Data were collected through questionnaires distributed to 100 respondents who met the criteria: consumers who had purchased or interacted with Cititex Kupang products through Instagram, were influenced by reviews or recommendations on Instagram, and had active social media accounts. Data analysis utilized simple linear regression and Moderated Regression Analysis (MRA). The results revealed that social media marketing significantly influences purchasing decisions at Cititex Kupang (t-count 9.733 > t-table 1.660; sig. 0.000 < 0.05). However, electronic word of mouth was not proven to moderate the relationship between social media marketing and purchasing decisions, as the interaction variable showed a significance value of 0.065 (> 0.05) with a negative coefficient direction, indicating a weakening tendency. This research contributes to the digital marketing literature by examining the moderating role of e-WOM in the relationship between social media marketing and purchase decisions, particularly in the context of local fashion retail in Eastern Indonesia, an area previously under-researched. The findings suggest that fashion retailers should optimize their social media marketing strategies through engaging content creation, active sharing, and community building. While e-WOM may not serve as a strong moderator, companies should still encourage customer reviews and testimonials to complement their promotional efforts. This study is limited to a single retail location (Cititex Kupang) with 100 respondents. Future research should expand the sample size, include multiple locations, and consider other variables such as price, product quality, and brand trust to provide a more comprehensive understanding of factors influencing purchase decisions.

title: The Developmental Trajectory of Growth Mindset and Its Longitudinal Associations with Internalizing Problems Among Chinese Adolescents
journal: Applied Research in Quality of Life
